entry.title: Re: iOS Build Failing
entry.content:
I generated a new p12 and provisional profile and still receive the same failed message:

AB version: 2.13.4
COMMAND: 

 npxrnv export -p ios -s standalone_appstore -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.167 -r --xcodebuildArchiveArgs "OTHER_CODE_SIGN_FLAGS=\"--keychain\ AppGyver-123250\"" 

FAILED with ERROR: 

Command failed with exit code 1: npx rnv export -p ios -s standalone_appstore -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.167 -r --xcodebuildArchiveArgs "OTHER_CODE_SIGN_FLAGS=\"--keychain\ AppGyver-123250\""

entry.title: Re: Form - Regular Expression Input Validation Message
entry.content:
Hi Joyca,

We faced the same situation last year when the feature was introduced. 

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/231967/?section=sectionComments

As a workaround we just edited the error message in the downloaded HTML code...of course that might not be an option especially if you are publishing the form and not downloading it and deploying it manually.

 

Kind Regards,

Markus

entry.title: Re: Deleting a Table from Excel Using ABAP2XLSX
entry.content:
If you wish to delete a whole tab, look into ZCL_EXCEL_WORKSHEETS (with S at the end).

The REMOVE method should be the one for you.

entry.title: Re: Creating A Simple Mobile Attendance Application With Local Storage
entry.content:
Hi Sankara1 

Noted with thanks!

Best regards

entry.title: Move Quantities in MF50 without consolidation of multiple planned orders
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

May i kindly seek for your help regarding our issue in MF50. Currently, whenever I try to move orders from one bucket to another using the move quantities along time axis icon, the orders are being consolidated. Such that if I have 2 planned orders in the same bucket. Say 40 and 50. When I try to move them, they are moved and a planned order with a quantity of 90 can be seen, instead of two orders.

Is there a configuration I might have missed? I have tried config of equal distribution, load on start date , load on end date but that's also consolidating quantities. It is very tedious to move every single order. Hoping so much for your help regarding this matter.

Thanks,

Jay

entry.title: Re: Where is SAP EA framework and methodology full description?
entry.content:
Thank you @NTeunckens

The pdf file is great, but seems outdated.
All others resources are not a detailed SAP EA TOGAF based methodology full description.

entry.title: Re: CAP application gives 401 unauthorized error after de...
entry.content: No, there doesn't seem to be any errors in the log.

entry.title: REFX- Flexible Real Estate Management- Interest Expense Calculation
entry.content:
Hello all, 

I just wanna ask how the interest exp in IFRS16 that is in REFX module calculated? What the approach ? Many of questions I have on it like based on what the interest % multiply is will multiply in clearing or dep. ? 

Please I am looking to full explanation from A to Z on how and what the approach used to calculate the interest exp. For the lease contract in refx. 

 

Thank you all!

entry.title: Re: FreeHand SQL query with input parameters
entry.content:
I believe SAC is not able yet to fully consume Odata parameters, hence you see this "Basic CDI Support" item on the product roadmap as 'future vision':

https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=67838200100800006884&q=odata&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=000D3ABE796A1EECB1F6529A576C2C99

entry.title: Re: ABAP BW RSA1 program name of routine
entry.content:
I found the answer I was looking for

RSA1-> locate the created routine -> click extras -> click Display Generated Program 

I found it from this blog

BW transformation debugging a start routine - SAP Community

Debugging ABAP routine Performance in SAP BW (element61.be)

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
Hi Alexander 

just edit the lic file and  replace the system number.

You can successfully install the license afterwards. 

Kind regards Ralf

entry.title: Re: OPS6/7: Text derieved from field PRPS-VERNR and PRPS-ASTNR is not changing
entry.content:
Hi @DE_Christian_H, 

this is standard behaviour, you can find a detailed explanation in Note 35972 - PSP: Name zu Verantwortlicher/Antragsteller - SAP for Me

In this note there is also a solution explained: Either you already have the report 'ZPSVERNA' installed on your system or you can create it according to the provied logic in the note via SE38. You can have someone from your development team involved, he/she can help you.

If this was helpful I would be happy to let me know it , e.g. as "accepted solution". 

 

Kind regards

Paul

entry.title: Configure Text Orientation using Annotation without Custom Column
entry.content:
Hi,

in our Smart Table, we have the requirement to have a string value oriented to the right side of a column.

As far as I understood, strings are always oriented to the left side of a column

We're currently only using annotations and I would like to achieve this without creating a custom column. Ist this possible?

Any Input is much appreciated!

entry.title: I am facing issues between S/4 and EPPDS
entry.content: For some material the operation status in EPPDS is Final confirmed but in S/4 it is showing Partial confirmed

entry.title: Re: Form - Regular Expression Input Validation Message
entry.content:
Thanks for your reply @MarkusGrant . Such a shame it was reported 1,5 year ago and nothing was done about it... did you ever make that second influence request that Sylvia asked for?

Unfortunately I cannot use your workaround, as we are using the Forms in a Landing page and publish it like that 😞

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Management Layer for SAP S/4HANA – Focus Topic: Deployment
entry.content:
Is it Mandated that you have to deploy EML on all systems in the landscape  - Dev/Qa and Prod

I do understand that the EML does not get upgraded when we upgrade SAP, what is the Process to keep EML up to date ?

does EML lose it compatibility when the system get upgraded say from S42023 FPS00 to a future release ?

 If we get EML on the latest version of S4 2023 FPS01 do we need to upgrade the connected systems for compatibility ?

entry.title: Re: XML Templating error
entry.content:
I have never seen a way to reuse a template.
For example I cannot access the link you provided:
http://schemas.sap.com/sapui5/extension/sap.ui.core.template/1

Is it a local XML? Can you use an external HTTP template XML in your code?

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup Tbilisi
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

You are invited to our very first pilot SAP Community Meetup in Georgia! 

  • Date: Tuesday, September 17th, 2024
  • Time: 18:30
  • Location: Rooms Hotel Tbilisi, 14 Merab Kostava St, Tbilisi 0108

We will have few short presentations, see agenda below. Then, we there will be time for informal networking.

Agenda:

  • 18:30 – 19:00: Networking: Casual chit-chat and mingling.
  • 19:00 - 19:15 : Opening by Keti Aladashvili, EPAM: Explanation of SAP Community Meetup's purpose and plan;
  • 19:15 - 19:25: Welcome speech by Valeri Sargsyan, Country Manager, Armenia & Georgia, SAP CEE;
  • 19:25 – 19:45: Presentation by Daniel Wroblewski and Shrinivasan Neelamegam, Developer Advocates at SAP: SAP Build overview, demo of Build Apps and Build Process Automation;
  • 19:45 - 20:15: Presentation by Natallia Shtura, EPAM: Exploring SAP Signavio Transformation Suite;
  • Informal Networking, Light food and beverages will be provided.

Join us for an evening of stimulating conversations and expanding your SAP network. Bring your ideas, questions, and business cards. RSVP now to secure your spot, as space is limited - please, kindly register via the link SAP Community Meetup (wearecommunity.io)

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP Optimization Help
entry.content: Please use the buttons "..." and "</>" to display your code nicely. See how to do it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/how-to-post-code-in-sap-community-gt-2024/qaq-p/13684774

entry.title: Re: Need an exit/BAdI while saving usage decisions in QA32/QA11
entry.content:
For those who is looking for exit - this badi is not called in RAP :

Das Business Add In (BAdI) INSPECTIONLOT_UPDATE wird beim Anlegen und Ändern von Prüflosen und beim Treffen und Ändern eines Verwendungsentscheids aufgerufen.

Beachten Sie, dass dieses BAdI nicht im RAP-Business-Objekt Prüflos (I_INSPECTIONLOTTP_2) verarbeitet wird.

entry.title: Re: How to check which configuration will be transported...
entry.content:
All changes made in the BCP (Business Change Project) will be transported to the Production system.

You cannot pick and choose what goes over. Additionally, there is no listing nor review of what has been configured. The configuration is made then pushed over.

Here is the link to the SAP Help document:
Configuring Your Solution | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Replicate transactions from one schema to another
entry.content:
Hi all, 

We have a request to replicate one of our schemas, and then at the end of each day replicate all the transactions that happened in the "original" schema. We cannot do a backup and restore as other data will be captured/imported into the "new" schema. 

Are there any tools/applications that can do this? Or does anyone have any ideas on how to go about this? 

Version: SAP B1 version 10, for HANA. FP 2208 

Thanks all,
Jerusha

entry.title: Request for SAP Customer Data Cloud - GDPR Delete Documentation

Hi Everyone,

I am seeking documentation related to the GDPR deletion process in SAP Customer Data Cloud that provides a clear and comprehensive understanding of the subject.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Best regards,
Ruchita

entry.title: Transforming Excel Files for SAP Uploads: A Smart Solution for Data Migration
entry.content:
In the context of SAP master data migration, I developed a VBA-based tool designed to automate the process of converting any Excel file into the correct format for SAP uploads. This tool is currently used for two key use cases: stock migration and packaging specification migration.

Use Case Example: Stock Migration

Consider the scenario where we receive an Excel file containing a customer’s stock data that needs to be migrated into SAP. Unfortunately, we can’t directly upload this file into SAP due to format incompatibility. Without automation, this would involve a time-consuming process of manually copying and pasting each row into the appropriate template.

The Solution: Automating the Process

With my tool, this manual work is eliminated. By simply pressing the “Start Migration” button, the VBA code automatically converts the input file into the correct format required for SAP upload, significantly reducing both time and potential errors.

Below, you can find a summary (non-exhaustive)



Benefits of the Tool

 

  • Automation: No more manual copy-pasting; the tool automates the entire conversion process.
  • Efficiency: The migration process is completed in seconds, not hours.
  • Flexibility: While currently focused on stock and packaging specifications, the tool can be adapted for other data migration needs.

If you’d like to discuss how this tool could help streamline your data migration processes, feel free to reach out. There are no limitations, only solutions! 😉

📧 h.evrard@cnt-online.com 📱 +32 473 60 82 70 🔗 LinkedIn: Hugo Evrard

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP Optimization Help
entry.content: (Run an ABAP Trace on your code with a small data set, and show us what is the top 10 of time %)

entry.title: Re: Planning your SAP TechEd sessions? Get insights from some SAP Champions!

And the most selected sessions by our sap Champions are:

AD200 Extend Joule with SAP Build and Develop Custom Enterprise Skills x2

AI180 Build your own generative AI scenario x2

entry.title: Re: XML Templating error
entry.content:
looks like you have no idea how templating works

please read the documentation.

https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/5ee619fc1370463ea674ee04b65ed83b

you have to preprocess it, before it can be used as a view.

entry.title: Re: Creating A Simple Mobile Attendance Application With Local Storage
entry.content:
Hello Johnny, 

Thanks for the confirmation.

entry.title: Re: iOS Build Failing
entry.content: Hello, Thanks for the confirmation.

entry.title: Re: Updating BrokerNOSource
entry.content:
Hi Jim,

This field is auto generated to link the Broker and Vendor together when the Broker is created using the Broker Entry Configuration. As you may know, this field in critical in calculating User Metrics to prevent counting the same Broker and Vendor twice.  From the Data Dictionary: Reference to the 'source' Broker. This is populated only for those Vendors that are automatically generated from the Broker or for Vendors that are synced during Broker posting. Use BrokerEntryConfiguration to enable this feature. This field is system generated and should not be user-editable.  In the past, we have used SQL Scripts to update the BrokerNoSource as part of User Metric Cleanups.  Hopefully this response will point you in the right direction....

entry.title: Failed to scribe a free SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi,

Already see the SAP Build Process Automation in Service Marketplace of my trial account for Sep. 12 course preparation. Double Click > Create > Free subscription plan > Failed (twice).

Kindly help. Thanks!

BR,

Sylvie

entry.title: Re: sales solution configurator
entry.content: Just to add: The ABAP add-on for ECC and S/4HANA is part of the SSC license. It does not need to be licensed separately.

entry.title: Reorganizing of demand with T-code MD74 is not working
entry.content:
Dear Exports,

I am reorganizing or deleting the old demand uploaded in the MD61 but I am unable to do so through T-code MD74, MD75, and MD76.

You can refer to the below screenshots for more details.

MD62

Reorganizing the same plan through T-code MD74 but getting a below screen and no data in the PBED table is becoming zero after execution.

Getting return message as "List doesn't contain any data".

PBIM and PBED table records you can see here.

Please help me to resolve this issue. This demand is not even being deleted through T-code MD74 and MD76.

Thanks in advance.

sincerely

Ali

entry.title: Re: sales solution configurator
entry.content: You can map the result of a solution configuration coming from SSC into a sales order without using a max BOM. Your order will contain a hierarchy of items and subitems according to so called relational characteristics in the SSC configuration result "is_part" and "has_part". If you then want to proceed to standard follow-up processes like production order, the system needs to map the SSC configuration result to a BOM. That happens automatically, but you need to create the max. bom.

entry.title: Business role provisioning from Microsoft Entra
entry.content:
Dear Gurus.

We are going to use Microsoft Entra as  Corporate Identity Provider for access to  BTP Subaccount and application. There are 2 points that need clarification.

1) How exactly we can create a role provisioning from Microsoft Entra to specific BTP subaccount?

2) When one user has different roles in different subaccounts. How the provision will distinguish between them?

I mean the same use Subaccount admin in the development subaccount and viewer only on production one?

Thank you.

Regards

Vladimir

entry.title: Re: Where is SAP EA framework and methodology full description?
entry.content: Well, it is a version of 2023, and it seems to be the most recent version that can be accessed via SAP-Help on the "Enterprise Architecture Framework" : SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,

Sven

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi community!
Happy to join the SAP Community. Looking forward to some interesting topics and discussions.
Kind regards,
Niclas

entry.title: Changing codepage for specific sapscript form?
entry.content:
Hello!

I have a question regarding an assignment from a client. Somehow, when they are using Hebrew text in a sapscript form, the Hebrew text gets reversed. We made it work, and reversed it, but now it turns out sometimes the text contains both Hebrew characters and Latin alphabet characters and numbers, which makes it a lot more complex. 
We'd kind of given up on solving the problem, but there may be one more possibility, which is changing the codepage. Because very likely the Dutch codepage we are using (may be using, I am not sure actually, I'll get back to that in a minute) doesn't contain Hebrew characters, but maybe the Hebrew codepage does contain Latin characters. 

However, i have no idea where or how to change the codepage. I wanted to do it right before the form is called in the program, but when I google around, I mostly find things about changing the codepage in the system, which I'm not sure they would want. 
I also found this post: Solved: How to change a Code Page in SAP SCRIPT ? - SAP Community. Here they mention "In unicode there is only one internal codepage, which contains all special characters of the individual languages.". And I looked at our system, and it's unicode. So that would mean that it already contains the Hebrew codepage as well, right? So changing it, even if I knew how to, wouldn't be of use because it's already there. 

Could someone confirm that that is right? And if it is not (or if it is), could someone maybe tell me what else I could do?

entry.title: Re: SetUserStatus Method
entry.content: Hi George, I don't have standard task 90400041 in my system (what's the BO associated with it?) - but you can look at both system statuses and customer statuses with transaction BSVW. There are many (many!) posts here about BSVW, so take a look around.

entry.title: Re: HXEDownloadManager can't run on windows 10
entry.content:
could you please try the following cmd on win10?
1. open PowerShell

$env:TEMP

show the system temp dir, I think it should be the path which was seen in the error you pasted.
2. you can try to modify this system temp dir
edit the system env variable TEMP, change to an existing dir.
or
you can set like this:

java -Djava.io.tmpdir=$YOUR_OWN_Existing_dir_TO_Keep_Log -jar hxedownloadmgr.jar

 Anyway, we will try to fix it in next release.

entry.title: Re: Making SAP Business One Web Client accessible externally using nginx as a reverse proxy
entry.content:
hi guy_sujetzki

Upto step 3 it is ok for me . but to  comeing to step 4 when I am change sld ip and authentication ip to public ip . I am getting error we can not found any https://external ip:8443 but when I am mention local ip it get successful updated but service got down .  We don't have domain name for  ip's. Is there any solution for this .

entry.title: Re: Request for SAP Customer Data Cloud - GDPR Delete Doc...
entry.content:
Hello Ruchita @Rvanjari,

I'm not fully sure what you mean by "GDPR deletion process" in CDC as GDPR is a standard process.

SAP CDC supports the GDPR-compliant deletion of personal data via API and also Brand provides options to right-to-be-forgotten.

To delete a customer's profile, you can also use APIs, DataFlows, or Screensets.

I hope this answers your query.

Thanks and Regards,
Kunal Bansal
SAP CX Solution Architect

entry.title: Viral TikTok Video Creates Cucumber Shortage In Iceland
entry.content:
The impact of social media on consumer behavior, and how it affects supply chain dynamics, cannot be underestimated. Case in point: a recent TikTok video of a Canadian influencer with a recipe for cucumber salad went viral and caused a shortage of cucumbers in Iceland. While other factors contributed to the shortage, TikTok is primarily to blame as farmers in Iceland couldn’t keep pace with the appetite of consumers eager to recreate the recipe in their own kitchens, according to this BBC article.

When a viral video triggers a surge in demand, businesses should ask themselves how they can stay resilient in the world full of disruptions. Let’s delve into some strategies to maintain a robust and adaptable supply chain in the face of unpredictable events.

Unleashing the Power of Social Commerce

Social commerce, the intersection of social media and e-commerce, has become a game-changer in the retail landscape. Statistics shows, that in 2023, sales through social networks accounted for an estimated 18.5 percent of total online sales. This figure is expected to continue growing in the coming years.

While many businesses have already started utilizing social commerce as a powerful tool to drive their sales, some are still struggling to keep up with the sudden spikes in demand that are caused by social media.

Modern supply chains can not afford to lag behind and that is why keeping a pulse on emerging trends becomes a necessity. By proactively monitoring social media platforms and staying ahead of the curve, businesses can identify potential demand-impacting trends before they hit the mainstream. This allows them to plan ahead, adjust their supply chain strategies, and ensure they're ready to capitalize on the next big thing.

Stay as cool as a cucumber

In the face of the recent cucumber shortage in Iceland, businesses can greatly benefit from harnessing predictive analytics to navigate the complexities of supply chain management. By analyzing a wide range of factors—including inventory levels, supplier risks, weather patterns, and market fluctuations—companies can proactively anticipate and prepare for potential challenges and disruptions. Demand forecasting and predictive analytics empower organizations to identify disruptions early and respond swiftly, fostering improved collaboration with suppliers.

By proactively identifying vulnerabilities in their supply chains and implementing strategies to mitigate risks, businesses can not only overcome challenges but succeed in the face of unforeseen events.

Emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), and cloud services are revolutionizing the supply chains as well. These innovative tools enable businesses to forecast demand more accurately and prepare for unforeseen events, be it a local produce shortage or a global event.

Modern world requires modern solutions

AI itself as a tool holds significant potential for predicting events and facilitating strategic decision-making. By integrating AI into their planning processes, businesses can enhance their ability to navigate the challenges posed by social media-driven demand spikes, like the one experienced in Iceland, to ensure a more resilient supply chain.

The Icelandic cucumber shortage is just a reminder that the spikes (or dips) in demand can happen in any place in the world. When a single social media trend can send demand skyrocketing, it becomes crucial for supply chain leaders to prioritize resilience and adaptability.

By embracing this challenge and harnessing the power of modern technologies, supply chain leaders can drive growth, innovation, and success in their organizations.

To discover key strategies for building risk-resilient supply chains, download the Oxford Economics Report: “5 Supply Chain Strategies to Achieve Resiliency and Avoid Risk.”

entry.title: Re: Making SAP Business One Web Client accessible externally using nginx as a reverse proxy

entry.title: Re: Request for SAP Customer Data Cloud - GDPR Delete Documentation

entry.title: How to trigger RAP Event in Unmanaged Scenario ?
entry.content:
Hello RAP Experts,

I need to add event on an unmanaged behaviour defintion. I have a button on list report and when the user clicks on the button , an event has to get triggered. In the case of managed behaviour defintion, there are few blogs which specify how this has to be done like using additional save in behaviour defintion and using save_modified  method in BP class and so on . 

But in an unmanaged scenario where exactly we have to write the RAISE ENTITY Event ? In the save method ? or one the action button method. Appreciate any help on this . 

@Andre_Fischer 

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model SAP BTP, ABAP environment

entry.title: Re: Tracking transformations by leveraging Architecture Decisions
entry.content:
Good point, Martin.

An enhancement to a specific fact sheet type might be an option. However, LeanIX is already working on a collaboration feature to support architecture decision making. More details are available here:

https://roadmap.leanix.net/c/372-collaboration-support-architecture-decision-making

entry.title: Configuration nodes for 4BL are not open in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
We are using 4BL as a best practice for integrating S4 Public Cloud latest version with ARIBA strategic suite.

We noticed that the S4 PERSON_NAME Public cloud (relevant nodes on CBC) is not open to configure the solution to integrate with ARIBA system via CIG.

We appreciate your supprot to solve this issue ASAP.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Configuration nodes for 4BL are not open in S4 Public...
entry.content:

Thank you for contacting SAP SUPPORT.

There are some Configuration which you need to follow that, open guide to perform those steps

For More info refer to the below KBA:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3211415

Thanks and Regards

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Re: My Inbox Fiori App : Configuring Open Task Navigation using SWFVMD1
entry.content:
Thx for the Blog.

Same question as Jens. How do you get the params?

I am trying to get TS00008323 - QualityNotificationTask to work but the QN Number is not getting transmitted.

Kinda lost what the syntax here is.

entry.title: Re: Details required on intelligent services
entry.content: This is likely not possible as the LMS identifies someone as a manager only if another LMS user's manager field has them listed. It's a dynamic query. There is delegate manager tables as well, but that is different. Maybe with this informaiton and some other values that are stored on Platform side you could make a custom field on the platform or integration center?

entry.title: FM writes file to al11
entry.content:
hi,

I need to write a .csv file to AL11 via report.

Can anyone tell me which FM i can use?

 

thank you

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Migration: Insights and Guidance for IT Leaders
entry.content:
As organizations worldwide continue to navigate the complexities of digital transformation, many that still rely on SAP ERP or the on-premises version of SAP S/4HANA are considering a major change: migrating to the cloud. This move isn’t just about a technical upgrade; it’s a strategic decision that could shape the future of your business. To help you get started, we’ve completely updated our guide, Mapping Your Journey to SAP S/4HANA, with practical insights, step-by-step instructions, best practices, and detailed background information.

Keeping up with the fast pace of digital change isn’t just beneficial — it’s essential. Industries like automotive, with their complex supply chains, clearly demonstrate the risks of not adapting quickly. That’s exactly why we created the original guide back in 2018, which quickly became a go-to resource for many companies. Now, I’m excited to share that we’ve just released a fully updated edition.

This edition brings together insights from dozens of SAP experts across the company, each sharing their unique perspectives and experiences. Inside, you’ll find a comprehensive guide that covers every aspect of the transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. We’ve focused on the key technology elements to help IT decision-makers confidently plan their roadmap, avoid common pitfalls, and ensure a smooth path to a successful go-live.

What makes this guide special is the wealth of knowledge it offers — drawn from thousands of successful transformation projects. It’s more than just a collection of tips; it’s packed with real-world experience, brought to life with clear text and visuals. Since every project is unique, we’ve included plenty of links to additional resources so you can rely on this guide at every step of your migration journey. The feedback from our customers speaks for itself: the guide has been downloaded more than 180,000 times since it was first published.

Why an update to our key reference

You may be wondering why a new edition, and why now? As one of the co-authors, I can tell you that while we’ve always kept the guide up to date, the past six years have seen major changes in the SAP landscape. New tools and methods have emerged, and our RISE with SAP offering has grown significantly. These developments have changed the way we help customers move to Cloud ERP, so it’s the perfect time to update the guide with these innovations and the valuable feedback we’ve received from our customers and partners.

Our goal with this guide remains the same: to give IT leaders everything they need in one place to successfully implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. We've carefully organized the guide into three key chapters:

  1. Strategic decisions

In the first chapter, we’ll help you make smart choices about the transition. We cover everything from deciding between public and private cloud options to understanding the unique benefits of the GROW with SAP and RISE with SAP programs, and even navigating the complexities of cloud operations.

  1. Ingredients for project success

The middle section delves into the critical factors that determine the success of your project. We discuss change management, knowledge management, customer and supplier master data integration, the partner ecosystem, and more.

  1. Essential tools from SAP

The final chapter provides an in-depth look at the 15 most important tools available to help you assess your systems, plan and execute the migration, and track project progress. These include SAP Data Services, SAP Readiness Check, ABAP development tools, and the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit.

More than just a guide, Mapping Your Journey to SAP S/4HANA is your trusted companion as an IT leader navigating a complex transformation. Whether you’re diving into the theory or need practical tips to keep your organization on track beyond 2027, this guide has you covered. You can read it cover to cover for a complete understanding or use it as a quick reference for specific stages or challenges.

I invite you to download it today and take the next step in your digital transformation journey: Download now

entry.title: Re: cms calendar
entry.content: the trigger-file must be created AFTER the scheduled starting-time

entry.title: Re: Announcing the Winner of "My Most Geeky Hobby” Contest
entry.content:
Thank you so much to the SAP Community team and to everyone who participated in the contest! It was a real pleasure to share my geeky hobby with you and to see so many creative and fun entries. I’m thrilled to have won second place with my idea of filling the office with action figures – because what better way to stay inspired at work than to be surrounded by our favorite characters!

Thanks to everyone who supported me and gave me kudos. I’m already looking forward to the next challenge!

Thanks,
Zami

entry.title: Re: Announcing the Winner of "My Most Geeky Hobby” Contest
entry.content: Congratulations @bhavinvyas !!! 😁

entry.title: Re: Deleting a Table from Excel Using ABAP2XLSX
entry.content: @abo Thank you for your response, but I don't need to delete a whole tab. Just I want to delete one table.

entry.title: Re: TS-Based Supply Optimizer finishes before Maximum Sol...
entry.content:
Hello Vincent,

the optimality tolerance is 1e-6 (0,000.001) in IBP supply optimization. If we encounter numerical issues (e.g. infeasible or unbounded problems) we raise the tolerances to 1e-5 as a mitigation option.

The average gap in product decomposition is computed via the sum of all objective values and the sum of all objective bounds over all subproblems. Don't quite see how we can end up in >2.7 ... e-05 in your case. For a deeper analysis we'd need an according optimizer diagnosis file (probably best via a support case).

We don't offer a parameter to influence the tolerances, as changes here rarely result in improved solutions and often can cause other issues (infeasibilities for example). If you see here issues, an option could be to involve our optimization expert consulting (see note 2427153 for more information).

Kind regards,
Carsten

entry.title: Re: Configuration nodes for 4BL are not open in S4 Public...
entry.content:
Dear Support team,

When we click on "open guide" it takes us to the maintenance planner page that doesn't contain configuration steps. Can you please advise.

entry.title: Re: PLMSI public cloud
entry.content:
Hello Martijn,

PLMSI is part of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition since version 2308, supporting also vendor management (sending vendor data from an external PLM system like Siemens Teamcenter to S/4HANA as business partner). The customer needs to have the license "PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition" and activate the scope item 6U2 (see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/4c05fe57755546318478dc7080daf0aa.html).

After creating a communication scenario, this functionality is enabled, together with the corresponding add-on on PLM side like "SAP Teamcenter by Siemens, Gateway for PLM system integration" (also known as "T4ST").

Best regards,

Thomas Elsässer, SAP Product Management

entry.title: SAP edocument - Support Documents (Documento Soporte - Colombia) XML
entry.content:
According to the documentation (DOC-SUPSAP_S4HANA_CLOUD ), we can create Support Documents (Documento Soporte - Colombia) using either materials or services. However, when processing a Service Entry Sheet (SES), the unit of measure is not being recognized, resulting in an XML parsing error.

How can we address this issue?

entry.title: Send Image from SAP Build apps to SAP DoX service via SAP BTP Destination
entry.content:
Hello experts,
my use case is I'm using Take Picture action in Build apps to take a Photo of Invoice and i want to Send the Photo to SAP DOX service via SAP BTP Destination as i'm  getting CORS issue with Normal REST APIs. 
My doubt is How to include the image as a Form-data in the post request??? 
or is there any better way to do This use case Please suggest i'm open for anything!!!

@AnuradhaDhingan 
@aman175105  @Chaitanya_Priya

entry.title: Re: Implementing 72-Hour Escalation in SAP S4HC Purch Req...
entry.content:
Hello @peter_munt4,

User can set up accordingly for planned and unplanned substitution, check the link below. This is the only possibility as of now.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/2c950e3d77504dd2a5dde48cdd4d43c4.html 

Otherwise, check for creating a situation template as referred to you in my last reply.

Thanks and Regards,

Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: How to check the GR volume and data per PO
entry.content: Check ME80FN. Change view to delivery schedule to get the receipt data. You can also select the view to display PO history.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI - From Postman to OData as a Receiver iFlow
entry.content:
Hi Avinash, 

at the step "Configuration of OData Receiver Adapter" I select "Local EDMX File" in the field Connection Source. After that, I click on the button Browse. But it returns No resource available. 

I change the Connection Resource to "Remote" and the Address is: https://services.odata.org/V2/(S(xjqjf2rkzrvecxualorv4hp))/OData/OData.svc/$metadata
The EDMX File can also not be found. 

Anything wrong? 

Thanks for your hints!

Regards
Rene

entry.title: Re: What Are You Looking Forward to Learning or Doing at Devtoberfest This Year?
entry.content:
I love the entire thing. I get to learn from experts and it's a great incentive for me to dive into the tutorials.

My goal this year, something I have not yet reached, Nerdvana....

I still have an unfinished crossword from a previous Devtoberfest that nags at me......

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m Samuel Bianchi, a Senior Product Marketing Manager at SAP Signavio in Barcelona. As Shreenith said, it’s great to be part of this community where we can all learn from each other. I’ve been at SAP Signavio for a while, focusing on helping customers get the most out of our products and ensuring they continue to see value in them.

I really liked what JD mentioned about mixing personal and professional experiences. I also think that building strong relationships is key to making changes work. That’s something I focus on a lot, whether it’s working with influencers and product advocates or listening to what customers need.

In my past jobs, I’ve worked on managing community platforms like this, and I’ve seen how valuable they can be. They help people share ideas, ask questions, and learn from each other.

I’m excited to join the discussions here, learn from you all, and hopefully share some tips from my work at SAP Signavio.

Looking forward to talking with everyone!

Cheers,

entry.title: Unable to modify the product once the follow-up sales quote is saved.
entry.content:
When creating a follow-up from a sales quote, the possibility of changing/searching products is missing 

Is this a bug?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to modify the product once the follow-up sales...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

When saving a new follow-up sales quote, the line items are automatically mapped between both sales quotes. This means that it is not possible to change the product using value help.

You need to change the product before saving the sales quote or you can remove the line item completely. 

This is a system standard behavior. 

 

 

Best regards,

Arjun T H

SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: Video Series - Developing side-by-side extensions with CAP for SAP Service Cloud v1
entry.content: Going to go through this weekend! 🙂 super nice content.

entry.title: Re: Deleting a Table from Excel Using ABAP2XLSX
entry.content: in this case, I suppose your best option is to loop over the rows and delete those in the table area

entry.title: Part 1: Change Management to Change Optimization
entry.content:
A word about me

Who am I? I'm a seasoned business development executive with a 26-year history at SAP and SAP Signavio. But I'm more than just a technology guy. I'm a historian, too.

My academic background in the History of Science and Military History has given me a unique perspective on change. I've studied how empires rise and fall, how society and human understanding evolve, and how businesses succeed or fail. And that understanding has shaped the way I see the world.

I believe that businesses, like empires, need to adapt to changing times. Many say that they need to anticipate trends, identify opportunities, and navigate challenges. But what goes unappreciated are the structures and drivers of change itself. That’s what this series of blog posts hopes to discuss. I hope to help businesses and, more importantly, Business Transformation Champions understand the forces shaping their industries and develop strategies to thrive in a rapidly evolving world.

A community call to action

This community of Business Transformation Champions intends to bridge the gap between process owners and enterprise architects. To connect, share insights, and learn together for successful transformation.  The key word here is “Successful”. 

Historically, ineffective organizational change management (OCM) has been cited as a major contributor to failed projects. While many individuals and organizations claim OCM expertise, the path to building a successful, repeatable change management practice remains elusive. Often, the answers are shrouded in ambiguity or claim to require years of experience.  As a community, we can break this pattern.  But how?

We need to adopt a more structured and systematic approach to OCM. Like the philosophical work of Antoine Lavoisier and Joseph Priestley, which transformed alchemy into the rigorous science of chemistry, we must establish clear frameworks, principles, and methodologies. By doing so, we can elevate OCM from a subjective, often haphazard practice to a disciplined and repeatable discipline. This will empower a thriving community of business transformation champions to drive successful change initiatives with consistent results.  But why now and where should we focus?

Change Management vs. Change Optimization: A New Era of Leadership

Inspired by Bob Evans and Thomas X. Hammes

I recently attended a keynote by Bob Evans on AI's impact on business. Inspired by his insights, I began reflecting on the implications for my field: change management. This blog post is the first in a series exploring these thoughts.

The business landscape is undergoing a radical transformation, currently driven primarily by the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence. Organizations are no longer defined by hierarchical structures but are flattening to become more agile and responsive. In this new reality, the distinction between change management and change optimization has never been more critical.

The Flattening Organization

Bob Evans, the astute observer of the tech industry and host of Cloud Wars, highlighted the flattening of organizations. As companies strive for speed and innovation, traditional hierarchical structures are giving way to more fluid, network-based models. This flattening trend presents both opportunities and challenges.

On the one hand, it empowers employees, fostering a culture of innovation and ownership. On the other hand, it demands new leadership skills and a fundamentally different approach to change.

From Change Management to Change Optimization

Traditionally, organizations have focused on change management, a process of controlling and directing change to achieve a desired outcome. While this approach is essential, it is no longer sufficient in today's fast-paced environment.

Change optimization, on the other hand, is about creating an environment where change is not just managed but embraced, leveraged, and continuously improved. It's about building an organization that thrives on change, rather than resisting it.

To illustrate the challenge of this transition, we can look to Thomas X. Hammes' book, The Sling and the Stone. Hammes describes the evolution of organizations through four generations. The first three generations were characterized by increasing complexity and bureaucracy. The fourth generation, however, is fundamentally different, emphasizing agility, adaptability, and innovation.

Moving from a third-generation to a fourth-generation organization is no small feat. It requires a radical shift in mindset, culture, and processes. It demands leaders who are not just managers of change but architects of transformation.

The Role of Leadership

To successfully navigate this transition, leaders must:

  • Embrace a growth mindset: Encourage experimentation, learning, and failure.
  • Build a culture of innovation: Create an environment where new ideas are valued and rewarded.
  • Develop change agility: Equip employees with the skills and tools to adapt to change quickly.
  • Leverage technology: Use technology to enhance collaboration, communication, and decision-making.
  • Measure and optimize: Continuously assess the effectiveness of change initiatives and make adjustments as needed.

Change management is still essential, but it's no longer enough. To thrive in the new economy, organizations must focus on change optimization. By embracing this shift, leaders can position their companies for long-term success in a world defined by constant change.

What are your thoughts on the shift from change management to change optimization? How is your organization adapting to the flattening trend?

entry.title: Re: Is there any filter for Q&A community by SAP products?
entry.content: Search is a big focus for adding in the managed tags as a filter right now and we are looking at 3 different options currently to find the best one.

entry.title: Re: iOS Build Failing
entry.content: Thanks for coming to my aid. Any feedback on what I could be doing wrong?

entry.title: Re: SetUserStatus Method
entry.content:
Hello, thanks for the contribution

The method is called SetUserStatus, and that's what the task is using. It is assigned to Object type BUS2005 but I ve also find it in other objects. 


After my research it is using the SAP Function Module BAPI_PRODORD_SETUSERSTATUS
This is what chatgpt is saying about it

From my understanding the job is done in binding of the task, because I cannot find how else I can insert the user status I want to be implemented.


The user status get predefined and then assigned to the object type. Maybe the first binding is not required.
I have pre-assigned the values of the status profile and user status having the same name as configured in the system.

In general, I want to understand how to work with this task. My goal is to automatically change the user status in a document after a user decision.
I tried to be as much detailed as I can. Thank you again

entry.title: Re: sales solution configurator
entry.content: hi thanks for all your inputs.

entry.title: Restriction for Formula Validator
entry.content:
How to impose restriction for all pages related to Formula Validator?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restriction for Formula Validator
entry.content:
It is possible to do so using Access Rights, specifically un-selecting Formula Debugger under Developer Tools tab.

This will prevent access to Formula Validator and all its associated tabs.

entry.title: Re: sales solution configurator
entry.content: just one more question, how is SSC different from The configuration and pricing services (CPS) on BTP

entry.title: Re: SetUserStatus Method
entry.content:
In continuation of my previous reply what I've realized is that the business object BUS2005 doesn't have an attribute for status profile or user status. Or maybe I cannot find it. So that's why I don't understand how I will be able to change this field in the object from the workflow (binding) if it doesn't appear.

For example the attribute order type can be modified by saying "bus2005.ordertype" but this might not be possible for a field such as user status and status profile. So maybe this task works a different way and I can assign the user status I want to change from somewhere else in this task.

At the end I want to use this function to change the user status for an inquiry, so if it works here I will ask for abap to duplicate it for custom object bus2032 that will be for inquiry

entry.title: Re: Employee Central - Compensation Promotion integration in Successfactors
entry.content:
Hello @xavierlegarrec 

Thank you for your answer. Another question. If we configure the Rule for Synchronizing Job Information to Position in Position Management Settings --> Synchronization, would this solve the problem?

So,

1. the managers/HR can use the Standard Promotion tab to edit the position or also change it with a new one. 

2. after the completion of the process, we publish the new position in Job Info

3. The question is: will the promotion publish coming from Comp Home trigger also the writeback rule in order to have the values of Job Info updated in Position Org Chart?

 

Thank you very muh for your time.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: List of Z objects associated with a program/Script
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have got a requirement that on searching I could not find an answer to it. 

Requirement:  I want a list of all the Z objects (data elements, Z tables etc ) used in my SAP Script. 
I have to manually go in and look for all the Z objects. Is there any way I can get the list of all the Z object used in my SAP Script/Program.

Thankyou in advance 🙂

entry.title: Re: Future Service Packs or Stack for Disclosure Management
entry.content:

Next Service Pack will be Stack 2200, to be released in November

I have no further updates on the ESRS taxonomy issue

 

Best regards

Marc

entry.title: Re: The number of thread at Os level is always grater than teh value reportetd in monThread table
entry.content: HI Andrea, I have the same question ..Did you get any answer for this? Thank you

entry.title: Allowing Absence and Attendance on the Same Day in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a requirement where we need to allow employees to both book absence and record attendance on the same day. For example, if an employee takes an absence on 06/09/2024 and their manager approves it, they should still be able to work on the same day and enter their worked hours in case of an emergency.

Does anyone have experience with this scenario or any suggestions on how to implement this in SAP SuccessFactors?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: The number of thread at Os level is always grater tha...
entry.content:
Hi

unfortunately  no

 

A.
